title: Cariniana excelsa
intro: Cariniana excelsa, also known as the white-shouldered tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lecythidaceae, native to the Amazon rainforest. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of various diseases and conditions. The bioactive constituents of Cariniana excelsa include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditional herbal preparations made with Cariniana excelsa include teas and infusions, as well as extracts that are often used in the treatment of gastrointestinal issues and respiratory problems.
taxonomy: Cariniana excelsa is a species of flowering plant in the family Lecythidaceae, commonly known as the Brazil nut family. It is native to the Amazon rainforest and is a large tree species that can grow up to 40 meters tall. Cariniana excelsa is closely related to other species within the genus Cariniana, which is characterized by its large, deciduous leaves and showy white flowers. The plant is also classified as a member of the order Ericales, a group of flowering plants that includes other species such as blueberries and azaleas. As a member of the Lecythidaceae family, Cariniana excelsa is part of a diverse group of plants that are found primarily in the tropical regions of Central and South America.

side_effects: Cariniana excelsa, also known as the Brazil nut tree, is native to the Amazon rainforest. While its seeds are edible and rich in nutrients, consuming large amounts can cause gastrointestinal issues. The shells of the seeds also contain a toxic compound called urushiol, which can cause skin irritation and allergic reactions in some individuals. However, the toxic compound is usually removed during the process of harvesting and processing the seeds.
